adaptation: Adapted brief, audience, asset, and constraint inference into a compact creative contract; avoided universal style values and long questionnaires.
Condensed macrostructure and structural-fingerprint concepts; removed recipe-specific and novelty-first absolutes.

Adapted generation-first direction exploration, whole-page macrostructure, visual-carrier hierarchy, type roles, color deployment, display crescendo, section choreography, and anti-house-style rotation without copying fixed themes or genre mappings.

Added a height-aware landing-page guard that budgets fixed chrome, required entry content, actions, and next-section reveal at short desktop and mobile profiles without imposing a universal font-size cap.
Reframed image-generation guidance as an optional asset loop for concrete subjects, with explicit limits against synthetic evidence.
Kept one focused visual refinement pass in normal generation and moved scoring, severity, and evidence-heavy review into explicit audit, evaluation, and production-hardening workflows.
Re-authored a smaller structured taxonomy with applicability, warnings, exceptions, and no executable lookup system.

Added generation-time guards for category shortcuts, per-section posterization, and repeated house-style bundles while retaining hard failures only for correctness, accessibility, honesty, and behavior.
Added explicit page-gutter, section-gap, panel-inset, stack-gap, and control-gap roles; included split-height balance and sticky-chrome clearance without imposing one universal spacing scale.

Required inspectable subject media for commerce and object pages, rejected abstract placeholders as primary product evidence, and clarified one-time labeling for fictional inventory, policies, and promises.
Normalized overlapping checklists into a smaller evidence-based gate set and aligned accessibility requirements to WCAG 2.2.
Combined mobile recomposition guidance with standards-based reflow, zoom, focus, and target requirements.
Added explicit untrusted-content, path, protocol, Markdown, and execution controls not treated consistently by the source skills.
